concerns.analysis: The source PDF is primarily a large, detailed GIS map showing sample locations, parcels, and topographic features. It also contains an agency logo in the top right corner. The HTML output contains no elements whatsoever; instead, it provides a plain text placeholder (`[Image: Site map of Southside Park Landfill...]`). Because this meaningful image (the entire map) and the logo are completely missing, this is a critical defect. Furthermore, the image manifest indicates a 0-length file for t
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Large GIS map of the Southside Park Landfill and the agency logo in the top right corner.
concerns.output_quote:
[Image: Site map of Southside Park Landfill showing surface water and sediment sample locations...]

(No actual element is present)

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): Drawing Path: T:\Raleigh-1050\Projects\2021\2115952_NCDEQ_So
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document uses underlining to emphasize the table headers (e.g., 'SW-1/DUP-6102025-SD' and 'ug/L') as well as the 'Risk Assessment' section within each table. This underlining is entirely lost in the output JSON, which presents the text as plain strings.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
